fact: May 20 is exactly 140 days into the year - that's exactly 20 weeks of perfect calendar math!
In the Northern Hemisphere, this is prime time for baby birds to be super loud and annoying as they beg for food from their parents all day long.
The sun rises before 6 AM in most northern places by May 20, which means even sleepyheads get blasted with morning light earlier than winter.
Dandelions are going absolutely crazy right now, shooting their fluffy seed parachutes everywhere and making wishes come true (or just making people sneeze).
May 20 falls smack in the middle of Taurus season, when the zodiac bull is supposedly making everyone extra stubborn about everything.
In Australia and other Southern Hemisphere places, this date marks the heart of autumn when leaves are turning brilliant orange and red.
Fireflies start waking up from their underground nap around this time in many places, getting ready for their summer light shows.
The Earth is tilted at just the right angle by May 20 that northern countries get about 15 hours of daylight - that's like having an extra afternoon!
Tree pollen reaches peak grossness levels around this date, coating cars in yellow dust and making people's eyes water like crazy.
May 20 sits exactly one month before the summer solstice, making it the perfect preview of the longest day coming up.
Thunderstorms love this time of year because warm and cool air masses are having epic battles in the sky, creating perfect storm conditions.
Garden snails and slugs throw their biggest slime parties around May 20 when the soil is perfectly moist and warm for their slimy adventures.
